Military


Ukraine Conflict News





30 November 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • U.S., Syrian Forces Locate and Destroy ISIS Weapons Caches in Southern Syria CENTCOM 30 Nov 2025 -- Forces from U.S Central Command (CENTCOM) and the Syrian Ministry of Interior located and destroyed more than 15 sites containing ISIS weapons caches in southern Syria, Nov. 24-27.

  • Trump pushing US toward 'foreign war' with Venezuela, top senator warns Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- US Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer has censured President Donald Trump for pushing the United States toward a "foreign war" with Venezuela.
  • Iran condemns US' move to close Venezuelan airspace IRNA 30 Nov 2025 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei on Saturday has condemned the US President Donald Trump's announcement of a closure of Venezuela's airspace, and called it a serious breach of international law and a threat to global aviation safety.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • PM call with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte: 30 November 2025 Gov.UK 30 Nov 2025

  • Norway Doubles Down on Ukraine War Machine, Teams up to Make Drones Sputnik 30 Nov 2025 -- Unfazed by the epic corruption scandal rocking the Zelensky regime, Norway's war hawks are ready to jointly produce Ukrainian drones.
  • Ukraine and Norway launch joint production of Ukrainian drones Ukraine MOD 30 Nov 2025 -- Ukraine and Norway will jointly produce Ukrainian drones. The agreement on this was signed by Denys Shmyhal, Minister of Defence of Ukraine, and Tore O. Sandvik, Minister of Defence of Norway.

  • China, Pakistan to hold 'Warrior-IX' joint counter-terrorism exercise: Chinese Defense Ministry Global Times 30 Nov 2025 -- From late November to mid-December, troops of the Chinese People's Liberation Army Western Theater Command and the Pakistan Army will conduct the "Warrior-IX" joint counter-terrorism exercise in Pakistan, the Chinese Defense Ministry announced in a statement on Sunday.
  • PLA Navy's Type 075 amphibious assault ship can carry Army's Z-10 helicopter: official media report Global Times 30 Nov 2025 -- The Type 075 amphibious assault ship of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy can carry the PLA Army's Z-10 attack helicopter, in addition to the Navy's own helicopters, air-cushioned landing craft and amphibious armored vehicles, according to an official media report on Sunday. An expert said that such cross-service joint operational capability can provide the Type 075 amphibious assault ship with more firepower.

  • North Korean military forces to gain 'strategic assets' amid heightened tensions in peninsula Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- North Korean leader Kim Jong-un says the air force will gain "strategic assets" amid the heightened tensions in the region.

  • Navy escorts Iranian ships in international waters: Commander IRNA 30 Nov 2025 -- Commander of the Iranian Army's Naval Force, Rear Admiral Shahram Irani, says that while escorts were once limited to areas near Iran, the Navy now provides escorts for Iranian ships worldwide.
  • Army Air Defense Force fully prepared to defend Iran: Deputy commander IRNA 30 Nov 2025 -- The deputy commander of the Army Air Defense Force says that the force has maintained full readiness for decades to defend the country in the face of hybrid threats.

  • Plane takes US trucks full of military equipment from UAE to Israel Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- An Antonov An-124 aircraft, carrying US Oshkosh trucks loaded with a large amount of military equipment, has arrived in the Israeli-occupied territories from the United Arab Emirates.

  • Japan's reported Type 03 missile export plan to Philippines breaks away from pacifist constitution, could undermine regional peace: expert Global Times 30 Nov 2025 -- Japan's Type 03 medium-range surface-to-air missile has again come under the spotlight, with Japan's Kyodo News on Sunday reporting that Tokyo is holding informal consultations with the Philippines over the missile's export. Amid Japan's consecutive military expansion gestures and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's erroneous remarks on Taiwan, a Chinese expert slammed the move as another breakaway from Japan's pacifist constitution that could undermine regional peace and stability.

  • Secretary-General's message on the Day of Remembrance for all Victims of Chemical Warfare UN Secretary-General 30 Nov 2025

  • PH, Japan hold 3rd maritime drill in WPS Philippine News Agency 30 Nov 2025 -- Filipino and Japanese naval and air units carried out their third bilateral maritime cooperative activity (MCA) in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) on Nov. 29, the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) announced Sunday afternoon.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • The Commander of the Northern Command in the Area of the Golan Heights-Lebanon Border Israel Defense Forces 30 Nov 2025
  • The Effort to Uphold the Ceasefire in Eastern Rafah Continues: New Footage From the Pursuit of Hamas Terrorists Israel Defense Forces 30 Nov 2025

  • Israel has killed 356 Palestinians in Gaza since ceasefire: Health ministry Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- Two more Palestinians have been killed by Israeli occupation forces in Gaza, raising the death toll to 356 since the ceasefire between the Tel Aviv regime and the Hamas resistance movement went into effect, the besieged territory's Health Ministry says.

  • Settler attacks 'part of Israeli policy to terrorize, displace Palestinians' in West Bank: Hamas Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- The Hamas resistance movement has strongly condemned the ongoing assaults by settler groups targeting Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, calling them as a deliberate escalation and an official Israeli strategy aimed at intimidating and displacing Palestinians.

  • UN condemns Israel's policy of 'organized torture' against Palestinians Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- The United Nations Committee against Torture (CAT) has condemned the Israeli regime for enforcing a policy of "organized torture" against Palestinians.

  • Statement by Foreign Minister Eide on the UN International Day of Solidarity with the Palestinian People Government of Norway 30 Nov 2025
  • General Secretariat commemorates International Day of Solidarity with the Palestinian People League of Arab States 30 Nov 2025 -- The General Secretariat of the League of Arab States is holding a central event at its headquarters on 30 November to commemorate the International Day of Solidarity with the Palestinian People.

    • Other Conflicts

  • UAE Strongly Condemns Israeli Strikes on Damascus Suburbs - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 30 Nov 2025 -- The United Arab Emirates (UAE) sharply condemned Israel's strikes on the suburbs of Damascus and called on the international community to stop repeated attacks on Syrian territory, the UAE Foreign Ministry said.

  • Record cocaine seizure in Haitian waters underlines country's 'pivotal' trafficking role UN News 30 Nov 2025 -- A record seizure of cocaine off the coast of Haiti has underlined the island nation's "pivotal role in trafficking routes linking South America, the Caribbean and the United States of America," according to the United Nations.

  • Iran issues ownership documents for Trio Persian Gulf islands Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- Iran has formally issued land demarcation and ownership documents for the islands of Abu Musa, as well as the Greater and Lesser Tunbs in the Persian Gulf, asserting its sovereignty over the territories.

News Reports

  • Russia's Baikonur Cosmodrome Damaged After Latest Soyuz Launch RFE/RL 30 Nov 2025 -- Russia's main launch facility for sending cosmonauts into space was damaged during the latest launch of a Soyuz capsule, prompting emergency repairs.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview with the Military Acceptance show, Moscow, November 30, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Nov 2025

  • PLA Daily article criticizes Japan's ambiguous, evasive defense of PM Takaichi's erroneous remarks on Taiwan question Global Times 30 Nov 2025 -- The PLA Daily, a newspaper affiliated with the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA), published on Sunday an article that criticizes Japan's ambiguous and evasive response to international criticism over Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's erroneous remarks on the Taiwan question, which appeared to be an attempt to gloss over the issue.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 30 Nov 2025

  • Splendid Celebrations Held to Mark 80th Founding Anniversary of KPA Air Force KCNA 30 Nov 2025 -- The Air Force of the Korean People's Army has defended the dignity and prestige of the great Workers' Party of Korea with absolute loyalty, heroic fighting spirit and matchless courage and firmly protected the inviolable sovereign airspace of the DPRK and the security of its people. It is celebrating its 80th founding anniversary in its history shining with victories and glory.

  • Speaker: Australia's anti-Iran move will fail to challenge IRGC's influence IRNA 30 Nov 2025 -- Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has strongly condemned the Australian government's action of blacklisting Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC), noting that the action will fail to challenge the IRGC influence.
  • Iran Army says Australia's designation of IRGC serves US-Israeli interests Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- The General Staff of Iran's Armed Forces has strongly condemned Australia's "unwise decision" to label the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) as a "state sponsor of terrorism."
  • IRGC seizes foreign-flagged vessel for smuggling fuel in Persian Gulf Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- The Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) says its naval forces have seized an Eswatini-flagged vessel carrying hundreds of thousands of liters of smuggled fuel in the Persian Gulf.
  • Iran a reliable energy supplier for Turkey, ready to extend gas export contract: FM Araghchi Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran is one of the most reliable energy suppliers for Turkey and is ready to extend a gas contract and enhance bilateral cooperation in the electricity sector.

  • Israeli PM Netanyahu formally asks for presidential pardon in corruption trial IRNA 30 Nov 2025 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has formally requested a pardon from President Isaac Herzog concerning the corruption cases he faces, the president's office said on Sunday.
  • Netanyahu asks Israeli president for pardon from corruption charges Press TV 30 Nov 2025 -- The Israeli regime's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, has asked the president to grant him a pardon from corruption charges.
  • Netanyahu Asks Israeli President Herzog for Pardon Amid Trial Sputnik 30 Nov 2025 -- The Israeli prime minister is currently facing charges for three cases of bribery, fraud and breach of trust.

  • Pakistan rejects the baseless statement by the Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Nov 2025 -- Pakistan notes with deep concern the ungrounded and misplaced apprehensions aired on behalf of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights regarding the 27th constitutional amendment adopted by the two-third majority of the parliament of Pakistan.

  • Leading Azerbaijani Opposition Figure Missing After Raids By Security Services RFE/RL 30 Nov 2025 -- Azerbaijani authorities detained the leader of the country's main opposition party after raiding his apartment, party officials said.
